body.title: [dependency] Retention tier for aged + superseded dev-process artifacts (noise-floor control)
body.description:
**Problem (Akien, 2026-06-27):** The canonical store accumulates artifacts that are past effective value. When decision A is later superseded by B, keeping A around a few weeks is useful — but very old superseded decisions and very old closed tickets raise the NOISE FLOOR of every grep/recall over the store. There is currently no retirement/archive tier; everything stays in the active dirs forever.
**Proposed shape:** A supersession-and-age-aware retention policy + mechanism: (a) when a decision is marked superseded, it ages out of the active set after a grace window (~weeks); (b) very old closed tickets age out similarly; (c) retired artifacts move to an archive tier — candidate destinations: an `archived/` subdir under each devlab/runtime/memory/<cat>/, OR the TheIgorsProject history repo (NOTE: TheIgorsProject is the intended history repository but is currently unorganized — 'whatever made sense at the time'; its own reorg is a separate, later effort and is OUT OF SCOPE here). Archive must stay grep-reachable on demand but be excluded from default recall. Preserve distinct records; never delete (archive, don't destroy).
**Affected files:** devlab/runtime/memory/** (new archive tier); recall/context-load readers (exclude archive by default); possibly memory_emit.py / *_store retention helpers
**Design rules:** One home no exceptions (archive is a tier WITHIN the home, not a new scattered location); preserve distinct records; check with Akien before any new ~/ path.
**Scope boundary:** Design + mechanism for retiring aged/superseded artifacts. Does NOT reorganize TheIgorsProject. Does NOT delete anything (archive only).
**Test plan:** A superseded decision older than the grace window lands in the archive tier and no longer appears in default recall/context-load, but is still grep-findable; noise-floor (active-store artifact count) measurably drops after a retention pass.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
